title: tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. hockii
intro: Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. hockii, also known as Hock's Tephrosia or Hock's bean trefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. hockii include its potential to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the leaves and stems being used in herbal preparations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
